title: Carl B. Metoyer Center for Family Counseling MODIFIED by John Wu
desc: Therapeutic counseling, crisis intervention, case management, and diversion for Oakland youth.

title: Catholic Charities of the East Bay
desc: Therapeutic counseling, crisis intervention, case management, and diversion for Oakland youth and families.

title: Centerforce – Youth Court & Restorative Justice
desc: Youth court for first-time juvenile offenders. Restorative justice education, parenting classes, and justice system education.

title: East Bay Agency for Children (EBAC) – Oakland
desc: Therapeutic counseling, crisis intervention, case management, and diversion for Oakland youth.

title: Fresh Lifelines for Youth (FLY)
desc: 12-week law education and life skills training. Leadership Training Program includes wilderness retreat, service learning, and coaching.

title: Safe Passages – Life & Career Roadmap
desc: Job readiness workshops, intensive career coaching, internship/work experience with financial incentives. Jobs 12+ months.
addr: 250 Frank H. Ogawa Plaza, Suite 6306, Oakland

title: Youth ALIVE! – Teens on Target
desc: Violence prevention peer educator program at Castlemont & Fremont High Schools. Speakers Bureau and Community Youth Leadership Council.

title: Youth Employment Partnership (YEP)
desc: Paid job readiness workshops and employment opportunities with nonprofits, government agencies, and businesses in Oakland.

title: Youth UpRising (YU)
desc: Integrated services: career & education, health & wellness, arts & expression, life skills. Drop-ins 9am–5pm daily. Social enterprise training.

title: Alameda County Crisis Receiving Home
desc: 24/7 emergency shelter for youth in crisis. Meals, hygiene, counseling, case management. NOT for probation or foster youth. Law enforcement contact required.

title: Community Works West – Restorative Justice
desc: Restorative justice and civic engagement for North County youth — accountability, repairing harm, and leadership.

title: Dream Youth Clinic – Cognitive Behavior Intervention
desc: Cognitive behavior intervention helping North County youth manage emotions and make positive choices.

title: EBAC – Life Skills & Support Services
desc: Life skills and support services: practical skills, financial literacy, education support, and independence resources.

title: Family Paths – Parenting Classes
desc: Parenting classes with practical child care, positive discipline, and family wellness skills. 24/7 Parenting Stress Helpline.

title: Oakland Natives Give Back – Gender Responsive
desc: Gender-responsive programming for young women — self-esteem, confidence, and empowerment in North County Oakland.

title: OK Program of Oakland – Mentoring
desc: Mentoring for African American boys — culturally aware adult guidance and positive role modeling. North County Oakland.

title: Positive Communication Practices – Group/Network Intervention
desc: Group and network intervention to break cycles of violence and gang involvement through counseling and conflict resolution.

title: Youth Spirit Artworks – Multi-Service
desc: Arts-based multi-service: anger management, civic engagement, cognitive behavior, and gender-responsive programming.

title: Youth Uprising – Diversion (JJCPA)
desc: JJCPA-funded diversion — early intervention to keep North County youth out of the juvenile justice system.

title: Berkeley Youth Alternatives (BYA) – Life Skills & Mentoring
desc: Life skills support and mentoring for North County youth — practical skills and supportive adults for independence.

title: Eden Counseling Services – Multi-Service (South County)
desc: South County: cognitive behavior, parenting, outpatient drug treatment, anger management, and diversion.
addr: 20253 Redwood Rd, Suite A, Castro Valley

title: ACOE Infant & Family Support Program
desc: Free early intervention for infants and toddlers with special needs. Part of California Early Start. No cost to families.

title: Butler Academic Center – JJC Education
desc: Accredited education at the Juvenile Justice Center — GED prep, college credits via Merritt College, special education.

title: Camp Sweeney Academic Center
desc: Residential education for adolescent males at the JJC. Individualized instruction, special education, and personal responsibility development.

title: Quest Academy – Independent Study (Grades 7–12)
desc: Alternative independent study for expelled, probation-involved, and foster youth in Alameda County. Oakland and Hayward locations.
avail: Referred by districts, probation, or child welfare

title: BOSS – Community Healing
desc: Community healing for families impacted by homicide and neighborhoods affected by violence.

title: Roots Community Health Center – Healing
desc: Therapeutic support for violence-impacted community — psychotherapy, healing practices, support groups.

title: Destiny Arts Center
desc: Healing circles, marches, vigils, and community dialogues that shift norms around violence. Financial support for homicide-bereaved families.

title: TRYBE – Neighborhood Community Team
desc: Visible community team — mediating conflicts, hosting neighborhood events, connecting people to resources after violence.

title: East Oakland Boxing Association (EOBA)
desc: Community healing in East Oakland for families impacted by violence — safe spaces and positive community connection.

title: CURYJ – Community Healing
desc: Restorative justice and community healing for youth and families impacted by violence.

title: Urban Peace Movement
desc: Community healing and neighborhood cohesion — healing circles, family support after violence incidents.

title: Restorative Justice for Oakland Youth (RJOY)
desc: Healing activities and financial support for homicide-bereaved families, plus community dialogues and restorative events.

title: Youth ALIVE! – Town Nights & Healing
desc: Therapeutic support for homicide-affected families and Town Nights — Friday evening community events in summer.

title: Ruby's Place – Domestic Violence Services
desc: Emergency shelter, legal advocacy, therapy, and transitional housing for survivors of intimate partner violence.

title: Serenity House Oakland
desc: Transitional housing for GBV survivors — up to 18 months of safe temporary housing and support securing permanent housing.

title: Family Violence Law Center (FVLC)
desc: Legal advocacy — legal advice, restraining orders, court representation for intimate partner violence survivors. 24/7 crisis line.

title: Young Women's Freedom Center
desc: Life coaching and safe space for young women who have experienced commercial sexual exploitation. Skill development and family strengthening.

title: BOSS – Gender-Based Violence Services
desc: Emergency shelter and resource navigation for survivors of intimate partner violence and commercial sexual exploitation.

title: Covenant House California
desc: Safe space for LGBTQ+ and gender-nonconforming youth who have experienced sexual exploitation — food, hygiene, support groups, rest.

title: East Bay Asian Youth Center (EBAYC) – GBV
desc: Bedside advocacy, accompaniment, and 24/7 crisis hotline for GBV-impacted individuals — culturally responsive for Asian communities.

title: Oakland LGBTQ Community Center
desc: Safe space and therapeutic support for LGBTQ+ individuals impacted by gender-based violence.

title: OUSD Office of Equity – GBV Support
desc: GBV support within Oakland Unified School District — resources and advocacy for students impacted by intimate partner violence.

title: Abode Services – Emergency Relocation
desc: Emergency relocation for individuals in immediate lethal danger from group violence — transportation, hotel, and initial rent.

title: Community & Youth Outreach (CYO) – Violence Interruption
desc: Violence interrupters respond to shootings and homicides to prevent retaliation and connect victims to services.

title: Community Works West – Youth Diversion (DVP)
desc: Youth diversion from the justice system through accountability and healing programs.

title: Safe Passages – Group Violence Life Coaching
desc: Life coaching and employment for individuals at the center of group violence — financial incentives, goal-setting, frequent contact.

title: East Bay Asian Youth Center (EBAYC) – Group Violence
desc: Youth life coaching and employment for youth at high risk of group violence — school attendance, graduation, and goals.

title: Lao Family Community Development – Employment
desc: Adult employment for high-risk-of-violence individuals — pre-employment training, paid work, job placement with retention support.

title: Center for Employment Opportunities (CEO)
desc: Pre-employment training and paid work for adults with justice involvement at high risk for violence. Job placement and retention.

title: The Mentoring Center
desc: Life coaching and mentoring for individuals at the center of group violence — positive relationships and referrals to reduce violence risk.

title: National Institute for Criminal Justice Reform (NICJR)
desc: Adult life coaching and case management for gun violence-centered individuals — individualized goal-setting and service navigation.

title: CURYJ – Group Violence Diversion
desc: Youth diversion and restorative justice for youth involved in group violence — alternatives to prosecution, accountability, healing.

title: Oakland Kids First – Youth Employment
desc: Pre-employment training, career exploration, paid work, and academic case management for youth at high risk for violence.

title: Oakland Private Industry Council (PIC)
desc: Workforce development for Oakland individuals at high risk of violence — job training, placement, and retention through employer partnerships.

title: TRYBE – Group Violence Life Coaching
desc: Youth and adult life coaching for individuals at the center of group violence — financial incentives and frequent contact for positive change.

title: Youth ALIVE! – Hospital Response & Life Coaching
desc: Hospital responders visit shooting victims and connect them to services. Youth life coaching for those at risk of violence.

title: Youth Employment Partnership (YEP) – Group Violence
desc: Youth employment — pre-employment training, career exploration, paid work, and academic case management to support school completion.

title: Roots Community Health Center – Group Violence
desc: Adult life coaching and culturally responsive health services for individuals at the center of group violence.

title: OUSD JJC – Youth Diversion
desc: School-based youth diversion through OUSD's Juvenile Justice Center — academic support and diversion programming.

title: Emergency Rental Assistance
desc: Short-term rental relief grants for households facing eviction due to job loss or medical hardship.

title: Small Business Launch Grant
desc: Up to $5,000 in seed funding plus free mentoring to help local entrepreneurs start or grow a small business.

title: Free Legal Aid Clinic
desc: Free consultations on immigration, landlord disputes, wage theft, and benefits appeals.

title: Adult Education & GED Prep
desc: Free GED prep, ESL instruction, and adult literacy programs with evening and weekend schedules.

title: Mental Health & Counseling
desc: Sliding-scale therapy, support groups, and crisis intervention in English and Spanish.

title: Food Pantry Network
desc: Fresh produce, shelf-stable groceries, and hot meals at community pantries across the region.

title: ACOE PACT – Positive Attendance Care Team
desc: ACOE's Probation-funded outreach team deploys Education Liaisons to homes of chronically absent or disconnected youth. Root-cause assessment, family support, barrier removal (up to $5K/student), and re-engagement planning.
avail: Internal referral via ACOE staff or school district

title: ACOE Opportunity Academy – Alternative Education
desc: ACOE's alternative diploma program for students 16 and older who are significantly over-age/under-credited. Flexible scheduling, credit recovery, and post-secondary preparation in Oakland and Hayward.
avail: Open enrollment — referrals from districts, probation, or child welfare

title: ACOE Community Education Space – My FUTURE Center
desc: Post-release education adjacent to My FUTURE Center for youth returning from JJC who are not ready for traditional school. Structured school day M–F 8:30am–2:30pm with meals, Special Ed support, literacy intervention, and after-school CTE/Laney College connections.
addr: Adjacent to My FUTURE Center, Oakland
avail: Referral via ACOE Education Liaison or JJC Transition Center

title: ACOE Parenting Teen Program (PTP)
desc: School and support services for pregnant and parenting teens to continue their education. Includes childcare coordination, health services, parenting classes, and diploma completion.

title: Covenant House California – TAY Shelter
desc: Emergency shelter, transitional housing, and wraparound services for youth ages 18–24 experiencing homelessness. Special programming for youth aging out of foster care. Food, hygiene, case management, job training.

title: Alameda County Social Services – Foster Care
desc: Alameda County child welfare services — foster placement, Independent Living Program (ILP) for youth aging out, Medi-Cal, CalWORKs, and education rights support for foster youth.

title: Building Futures – TAY Transitional Housing
desc: Transitional housing and supportive services for young adults 18–25 in South County, including those aging out of foster care. Rent assistance, case management, and life skills.

title: Abode Services – Youth Rapid Rehousing
desc: Rapid rehousing and rental assistance for unhoused young adults, with priority for youth aging out of foster care. Security deposit, first/last month's rent, and ongoing case management.

title: Oakland Public Library – Community Learning Centers
desc: Free tutoring, homework help, GED prep, digital literacy, and adult education at branches across Oakland. Safe drop-in spaces and library card access for all residents.

title: Laney College – Dual Enrollment & Credit Recovery
desc: Dual enrollment for high school students to earn college credits, plus credit recovery courses and vocational certificates. Free tuition for eligible students. Strong CTE pathway connections.
avail: Semester enrollment — open to dual enrollment by referral

title: Oakland USD – Linked Learning & CTE Pathways
desc: OUSD's career-connected learning pathways — Health, Engineering, Arts, Business, and Skilled Trades. Real-world projects, internships, and industry credentials alongside diploma credits. Strong re-engagement hook for students unmotivated by traditional academics.
avail: School enrollment required — re-engagement pathway available

title: Eden ROP – Career & Technical Education
desc: Free CTE courses in Skilled Trades, Health Careers, IT, and Business for Alameda County students and young adults. Industry certifications, paid internships, and articulation to community college. South County focus.

title: Alameda County Community Food Bank
desc: Free groceries, produce, and emergency food boxes at over 350 distribution sites across Alameda County. No documentation required. Home delivery available for some areas.
avail: Distribution sites open countywide — find nearest at accfb.org

title: CalFresh / SNAP Food Benefits
desc: California's food assistance program. Eligible households receive monthly EBT benefits for groceries. Youth 18+ may qualify independently. Fast application available online. Many immigrant families qualify.
addr: Online application or in-person at ACSSA offices
avail: Apply anytime — benefits typically start within 30 days

title: Marin/Sonoma/Napa Street Violence – WIC
desc: WIC (Women, Infants & Children) provides free nutritious foods, nutrition education, breastfeeding support, and healthcare referrals for pregnant women, new mothers, infants, and children up to age 5.

title: A Step Forward, Inc.
desc: Behavioral health services and population-specific programs for at-promise and justice-involved youth in Berkeley and countywide.

title: Alameda County Behavioral Health – CYASOC
desc: County mental health system of care for children and youth. Therapeutic services, crisis intervention, case management, and life skills for at-promise and justice-involved youth across all ages.
avail: County referral process — contact for intake

title: Alameda County Day Reporting Center
desc: Comprehensive reentry day program for justice-involved young adults 18–25. Academic support, job training and placement, case management, housing navigation, life skills, and basic needs — all in one location. Operated by CenterPoint Inc.

title: Alameda County Family Justice Center
desc: One-stop center for survivors of domestic violence, sexual assault, and other crimes. On-site services include basic needs, behavioral health, housing advocacy, legal services, and restorative justice. Countywide.
avail: Walk-in and referral — open to all survivors

title: Alameda County Victim-Witness Assistance
desc: Alameda County DA office program providing crisis intervention, behavioral health support, and legal navigation for crime victims and their families. Countywide services, all victims served.
avail: Open to all crime victims — walk-in and referral

title: CASA of Alameda County
desc: Court Appointed Special Advocates — trained volunteer mentors for youth in foster care and the justice system. Provides guidance, advocacy, and consistent trusted adult relationships. Countywide.
addr: 1000 San Leandro Blvd, Suite 300, San Leandro, CA 94577
avail: Court referral or CWS referral required

title: Alameda County Bar Association – Legal Services
desc: Legal education, restorative justice, and diversion programming for at-promise and justice-involved youth ages 6–17. Countywide.
addr: 548 Market Street PMB 22692, San Francisco, CA 94104

title: Bay Area Legal Aid
desc: Free civil legal services for low-income youth and adults — housing, immigration, public benefits, and domestic violence legal advocacy. Countywide Alameda County.

title: Banteay Srei – Asian Youth & Women
desc: Culturally grounded life skills, mentoring, and prevention for Southeast Asian girls and young women in Oakland. Addresses trafficking, sexual exploitation, and gender-based harm with culturally responsive programming.

title: Center for Empowering Refugees & Immigrants (CERI)
desc: Behavioral health, life skills, mentoring, and culturally responsive programming for refugee and immigrant youth across Alameda County. Addresses trauma, acculturation, and community belonging.

title: Choices for Freedom Inc.
desc: East Oakland-based academic support, housing navigation, physical health, life skills, recreation, and restorative justice for at-promise and justice-involved youth ages 6–17.

title: Fred Finch Youth & Family Services
desc: Comprehensive mental health, housing, case management, career training, life skills, and crisis services for at-promise and justice-involved youth across Alameda County. Deep experience with foster youth.

title: Girls Inc of Alameda County
desc: Programming for girls and young women — academic support, behavioral health, career training, life skills, physical health, and recreation. Countywide with Oakland focus. Empowerment and education centered.

title: Greater New Beginnings
desc: Oakland-based comprehensive services for justice-involved youth ages 6–17: basic needs, housing, case management, crisis support, academic support, career readiness, and life skills.

title: H.A.A.R.T – Humanistic Alternatives to Addiction
desc: Oakland-based behavioral health, case management, crisis intervention, life skills, and physical health services for at-promise youth 18–25 with substance use and co-occurring needs.

title: Jewish Family & Community Services East Bay
desc: Comprehensive services countywide: behavioral health, basic needs, housing, career training, case management, crisis, and legal support for youth and families regardless of background or religion.

title: La Clinica de la Raza
desc: Culturally competent health and mental health services for Oakland and Fruitvale communities. Basic needs, behavioral health, case management, housing navigation, life skills, and primary care for all ages.
avail: Open — sliding scale and Medi-Cal accepted

title: La Familia Counseling Center
desc: Comprehensive behavioral health, academic support, career training, case management, and life skills for youth and families in Hayward and countywide. Bilingual services. Probation employment contact: MOgundare@LiveLaFamilia.org | (510) 300-3111.

title: Lincoln Families
desc: Mental health, case management, crisis intervention, life skills, and academic support for youth and families across Alameda County. Serves at-promise and justice-involved youth of all ages.

title: Men of Valor Academy
desc: Oakland-based program for justice-involved young men 18–25. Academic support, behavioral health, career training, housing, and life skills. Navigating Chance reentry program.

title: Millie’s Youth Services
desc: Comprehensive services for at-promise and justice-involved youth in East Oakland: basic needs, housing, case management, crisis support, behavioral health, academic support, and life skills.

title: Misssey Inc – Commercial Sexual Exploitation
desc: Oakland-based specialized services for commercially sexually exploited children and youth. Housing, behavioral health, life skills, and restorative justice for youth ages 6–25, with focus on girls and LGBTQ+ youth.

title: Native American Health Center
desc: Culturally grounded behavioral health, physical health, crisis intervention, and population-specific programs for Native American and all youth in Oakland. Holistic wellness and traditional healing approaches.

title: Our SafeHaven Inc.
desc: Castro Valley-based comprehensive services for at-promise and justice-involved youth: basic needs, behavioral health, crisis support, life skills, restorative justice, and academic support. Countywide.

title: R.E.F.U.G.E Family Services
desc: East Oakland behavioral health and case management services for at-promise and justice-involved youth ages 6–17 and their families. Trauma-informed, culturally responsive.

title: REACH Ashland Youth Center
desc: San Leandro-based comprehensive youth center: academic support, basic needs, behavioral health, career training, life skills, and physical health for at-promise youth. BACR operated.

title: Restorative Pathways
desc: Castro Valley-based restorative justice, academic support, behavioral health, case management, housing assistance, and life skills for at-promise and justice-involved youth. Countywide reach.

title: Safe Alternatives to Violent Environments (SAVE)
desc: Fremont-based domestic violence services: emergency shelter, behavioral health, crisis intervention, life skills, basic needs, and prevention for survivors in South Alameda County.

title: Seneca Family of Agencies
desc: Comprehensive mental health, housing, case management, crisis, and life skills services for at-promise, justice-involved, and foster youth across all ages in Alameda County. YSC case management contact: AlamedaICMReferrals@senecacenter.org (510) 504-6836.

title: Terra Firma Diversion
desc: Hayward-based behavioral health, case management, life skills, and restorative justice diversion for at-promise and justice-involved young adults 18–25. South County focus.

title: Thunder Road Adolescent Treatment Center
desc: Intensive outpatient and residential substance use treatment for adolescents and young adults 13–25. Dual diagnosis care for co-occurring mental health needs. Oakland.
avail: Contact for intake — insurance and Medi-Cal accepted

title: WestCoast Childrens’s Clinic
desc: Behavioral health and trauma-informed mental health services for children, youth, and families across Alameda County. Serves foster youth, justice-involved youth, and others with complex trauma histories.

title: Youth Advocate Programs (YAP)
desc: Intensive community-based advocacy and crisis response for justice-involved young adults. Life skills, behavioral health, crisis intervention, and restorative justice alternatives to incarceration. Countywide.

title: Alameda Family Services
desc: Alameda city-based behavioral health, basic needs, crisis intervention, and physical health for youth and families. YSC therapeutic counseling: bhcs@alamedafs.org | (510) 629-6364.
addr: 2325 Clement Ave, Suite A, Alameda, CA 94501

title: City of Fremont – Youth & Family Services
desc: Fremont city-operated behavioral health and counseling for youth and families. YSC therapeutic and case management services. Probation referrals: yfs@fremont.gov | (510) 675-5817.
addr: 39155 Liberty St, Suite E500, Fremont, CA 94538
avail: Probation and school referrals — contact for intake

title: City of Hayward – Youth & Family Services Bureau
desc: Hayward city services: behavioral health, case management, crisis, diversion, gender-responsive programming, and restorative justice for Hayward youth. YSC and CP services. Contact: yfsb@hayward-ca.gov | (510) 293-7048.

title: City of Union City – Youth & Family Services
desc: Union City youth services: behavioral health, career training, crisis intervention, life skills, diversion, and gender-responsive programming for Fremont/Newark/Union City youth. Contact: YFS@unioncity.org | (510) 675-5217.

title: City of Livermore – Horizons Family Counseling
desc: Livermore city counseling for youth and families in the Tri-Valley. Case management, crisis intervention, and therapeutic support for Dublin, Livermore, Pleasanton, and Sunol. Contact: mtemores@livermoreca.gov | (925) 371-4742.

title: Crisis Support Services of Alameda County
desc: 24/7 crisis hotline and mobile crisis response for individuals in mental health crisis. Connects to emergency services, crisis counseling, and next-step resources. Free and confidential.

title: Bay Area Crisis Nursery
desc: Free, voluntary crisis childcare for children birth–5 when families face temporary crises (hospitalization, domestic violence, homelessness). Up to 30 days. No referral needed.
